nodes.body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Canonicalize allowed actions before hashing diagnosis report**

The safe-mode report is documented as deterministic, but `next_allowed_actions` is serialized directly from `input.allowedActions` without canonical ordering, so the same logical action set produces different `reportHash` values when callers provide a different order (for example, actions aggregated from a map/set or UI reorder). This breaks hash stability for equivalent diagnoses and can cause false drift/continuity mismatches.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Return cloned repair actions to avoid shared-state mutation**

`actionsForSignal` returns references from `REPAIR_ACTION_LIBRARY` instead of copies, so any caller-side mutation (for example, appending to `commandPlan` for display or instrumentation) mutates global shared objects and changes subsequent calls. This introduces cross-request state leakage and non-deterministic remediation plans over time.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Validate loop threshold before slicing detection window**

When `threshold` is `0` (or negative), `history.length < threshold` is false and `window` can be empty, but `loopSignature(window[0])` is still called, which throws at runtime (`Cannot read properties of undefined`). If this threshold is configurable, one bad value can crash health evaluation instead of returning a normal signal/null result.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
